message:
Fix cargo-dist workspace metadata and add repository URL

Corrects the cargo-dist configuration structure by migrating from the
deprecated package.metadata.dist to the proper workspace.metadata.dist
namespace, which is required for cargo-dist 0.4+ when using workspace
mode.

Changes:
- Move dist metadata from [package.metadata.dist] to [workspace.metadata.dist]
- Add explicit targets array: ["x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"]
- Add explicit installers array: ["shell", "powershell"] (replacing boolean flag)
- Add repository URL to package metadata for cargo publish compatibility

The workspace.metadata.dist approach allows cargo-dist to properly generate
release artifacts and installers for all workspace members (even single-crate
workspaces benefit from this structure for future extensibility).

The targets field restricts initial release builds to Linux x86_64, which
aligns with the project's primary deployment platform. Additional targets
(macOS arm64, Windows x86_64) can be added incrementally after validating
the release pipeline.

The repository URL enables cargo registry publication and provides proper
source links in generated documentation and crates.io listings.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6,6 +6,7 @@ authors = ["Coding Agent Team"]
 description = "Unified TUI search over local coding agent histories"
 license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
 resolver = "2"
+repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search"
 
 [dependencies]
 anyhow = "*"
@@ -57,6 +58,7 @@ strip = true
 panic = "abort"
 opt-level = "z"
 
-[package.metadata.dist]
-installer = true
+[workspace.metadata.dist]
+targets = ["x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"]
+installers = ["shell", "powershell"]
 ci = ["github"]
